sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: AA+, Moody's: Aa3, Fitch: NR

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Taiwan has the lowest energy independence in the dataset at 5%, making it extremely vulnerable to the oil price surge and Hormuz disruption. Cross-strait tensions remain elevated after China's PBOC FX policy shift and ongoing military posturing. A $10B+ US arms deal signals continued defense commitment. US tariff escalation adds export pressure for semiconductor-heavy economy. TSMC remains globally critical for AI chip supply.
key_changes_this_week: Oil +13% surge creates extreme import cost pressure (energy independence: 5%) (warning)
US-Taiwan $10B+ arms deal signed amid cross-strait tensions
China PBOC FX shift signals tolerance for yuan weakening -- competitive pressure on Taiwan exports
Japan stated SDF could intervene if China acts militarily on Taiwan
narrative.outlook: Taiwan's strategic importance as the global semiconductor hub continues to generate both economic opportunity and geopolitical risk. Energy vulnerability is the immediate concern with oil prices potentially reaching $100/barrel. China's NPC (March 5) and Trump-Xi summit (March 30) are key dates for cross-strait dynamics. TSMC's position in AI chip supply chains provides structural support but also makes Taiwan a focal point for great power competition.
narrative.investor_implications: TSMC remains a core holding for tech-exposed portfolios but geopolitical risk discount should be maintained. TAIEX faces headwinds from energy costs and China tensions. Taiwan sovereign bonds (AA/Aa3) provide safety. Semiconductor sector may decouple from broader market on AI fundamentals. Monitor NPC and Trump-Xi summit for cross-strait signals.
narrative.data_quality_note: Taiwan macro data is high quality. Energy independence is a derived metric. Arms deal values are from media reporting. Cross-strait risk assessment is qualitative.

active_alerts.title: Taiwan highly exposed to oil price surge (energy independence: 5%)
active_alerts.description: Oil prices surged 13% following the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse. Taiwan has an energy independence index of only 0.05, making it highly vulnerable to sustained energy price increases. Brent crude forecast to spike to $100/barrel if conflict persists. This threatens inflation, trade balance, and GDP growth for energy-import-dependent economies.

active_alerts.title: Taiwan faces unprecedented Chinese military escalation with blockade simulation and airspace violation
active_alerts.description: China conducted blockade simulation exercises (Justice Mission 2025) around Taiwan Dec 29-30. PLA drone flew through Taiwanese airspace over Pratas Island on Jan 17 -- possibly first confirmed PLA violation in decades. Opposition parties blocked asymmetric defense budget for 6th time. US-Taiwan $10B+ arms package announced but US hesitating on delivery ahead of Trump's Beijing visit. Chokepoint exposure at 0.69, energy independence at only 0.05.
